nodes.label: Supplier Disruption Response Protocol
nodes.description: When a tier-1 supplier is flagged for risk, trigger impact assessment and alternative sourcing evaluation.

nodes.label: Alternative Sourcing Evaluation
nodes.description: Score alternative suppliers on lead time, quality qualification status, cost delta, and sanctions compliance.

nodes.label: Price Hedge Trigger
nodes.description: When commodity price exceeds threshold, evaluate hedging options and contract renegotiation.

nodes.label: DRC Cobalt Disruption Playbook
nodes.description: Mined from 5 past DRC disruptions: switch to Australian cobalt within 14 days (qualified), but expect 22% cost increase. Always pre-position 6 weeks of cobalt buffer stock.
nodes.creator: people-sc-vp

nodes.label: Lithium Price Hedge Trigger
nodes.description: Taught by VP: When lithium carbonate spot price exceeds $25/kg, lock in 6-month forward contracts immediately. We got burned in 2022 waiting.
nodes.creator: people-sc-vp

nodes.label: Sanctions Compliance Quick Check
nodes.description: Learned from correction: Always check OFAC SDN list before qualifying any new nickel supplier - we almost onboarded a sanctioned entity in 2024.
nodes.creator: people-procurement-lead

nodes.label: Critical Material Dual-Source Rule
nodes.description: Any material in 3+ product lines must have at least 2 qualified suppliers. No exceptions.
nodes.creator: people-sc-vp

nodes.label: Supply Chain VP
nodes.description: Executive level. Sets supply chain strategy, manages critical supplier relationships.

nodes.label: Procurement Lead
nodes.description: Individual level. Handles supplier qualification, sourcing, and cost negotiations.

nodes.label: Procurement
nodes.description: Manages supplier relationships, sourcing strategy, and procurement operations.

nodes.label: Risk Management
nodes.description: Monitors and mitigates supply chain, financial, and operational risks.

nodes.label: IRA Domestic Content
nodes.description: Inflation Reduction Act domestic content requirements for battery manufacturing tax credits

nodes.label: EU Battery Passport
nodes.description: EU regulation requiring digital product passport with full supply chain traceability

nodes.label: CBAM
nodes.description: Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism - embedded emissions reporting for imported materials

nodes.label: Compliance Gap Remediation
nodes.description: When a regulation changes, scan all product lines for new gaps and generate remediation plans with cost estimates.

nodes.label: Regulation Change Assessment
nodes.description: When a new regulation or amendment is published, map it to affected products, materials, and suppliers.

nodes.label: IRA Content Calculation Shortcut
nodes.description: Learned from correction: The IRA domestic content calculation uses the manufacturing cost method, not the weight method. Weight method overestimates compliance by 10-15%.
nodes.creator: people-compliance-dir

nodes.label: EU Battery Passport Data Mapping
nodes.description: Taught by Compliance Director: For the EU Battery Passport, carbon footprint data must be traced back to smelter level, not just refiner. Most teams miss this.
nodes.creator: people-compliance-dir

nodes.label: Compliance Director
nodes.description: Department level. Manages regulatory compliance, audit preparation, and reporting across all battery product lines.

nodes.label: Legal & Compliance
nodes.description: Manages regulatory compliance, legal affairs, and audit preparation.

questions.question: DRC cobalt mine just had a cave-in. 60-day production halt confirmed. What do we do?
questions.answer: Cave-in affects 3 product lines through Cobalt Sulfate feeding the Cathode Precursor Plant in Finland. The DRC Cobalt Playbook recommends immediate switch to Australian cobalt (14-day lead time, qualified, +22% cost). 6-week buffer stock should be pre-positioned. Forward contracts recommended before spot price spikes. Supply Chain VP reviews cost impact; Procurement Lead executes the supplier switch.
reasoningSteps: Disruption Response Protocol triggers on cobalt supplier.

Entity traversal: Cobalt Mining Corp (sup-02) -> Cobalt Sulfate (ing-02) -> Cathode Precursor Plant Finland -> Cell Assembly Nagoya -> NMC811 Pouch Cell -> Gigafactory Nevada -> EV Battery Pack + Grid Storage Module.
Skill fires: "DRC Cobalt Disruption Playbook" (Executive priority) - switch to Australian source (qualified), expect 22% cost increase, pre-position 6 weeks buffer.
Alternative Sourcing Evaluation triggers: Australian source scored on lead time, qualification, cost, sanctions.
Skill fires: "Dual-Source Rule" confirms Australian source is already qualified.
Skill fires: "Sanctions Compliance Quick Check" - Australian source cleared on OFAC SDN.
Price Hedge Trigger checks: cobalt spot price likely to spike. Recommend locking forward contracts now.
People routed: Supply Chain VP gets executive summary with cost impact. Procurement Lead gets supplier switch execution checklist.

questions.question: If Cobalt Mining Corp in the DRC faces a 60-day production halt, which products and customers are affected?
questions.answer: Cobalt Mining Corp supplies Cobalt Sulfate, which feeds into the Cathode Precursor Plant in Finland. This plant also requires Lithium Carbonate and Nickel Sulfate - it's a critical dependency. The precursor plant feeds Cell Assembly in Nagoya, which manufactures NMC811 Pouch Cells. These cells feed Gigafactory Nevada, producing both EV Battery Pack (for Tesla and European Auto OEM) and Grid Storage Module (for Grid Operator X). The entire downstream product line is at risk.
reasoningSteps: Cobalt Mining Corp supplies Cobalt Sulfate
Cobalt Sulfate feeds Cathode Precursor Plant in Finland
Cobalt Mining has a critical dependency on this plant
Precursor Plant feeds Cell Assembly in Nagoya
Nagoya manufactures NMC811 Pouch Cells
Pouch Cells feed Gigafactory Nevada
Nevada produces EV Battery Packs and Grid Storage Modules
Tesla, European Auto OEM, and Grid Operator X all impacted

questions.question: Graphite International is our only graphite supplier. What happens if they can't deliver?
questions.answer: Graphite International from China is flagged as a single-source risk for Cell Assembly in Nagoya. They supply Graphite Anode Material, which feeds directly into the Nagoya cell assembly line. Without graphite, NMC811 Pouch Cell production stops, cascading to Gigafactory Nevada and all downstream products. No alternative supplier exists in the current graph - this is a critical procurement gap.
reasoningSteps: Graphite International is flagged as single-source risk for Nagoya
Supplies Graphite Anode Material
Anode material feeds directly into Cell Assembly Nagoya
No alternative supplier exists in the system
Production halt cascades to NMC811 cells, then Gigafactory
Both EV Battery Packs and Grid Storage Modules affected

questions.question: Lithium carbonate prices spike 30% on the spot market. What's the portfolio impact?
questions.answer: Lithium Carbonate is supplied by Lithium Supplier A (Chile) and also procured through the Spot Market. It co-requires Nickel Sulfate for cathode synthesis. Both materials feed Cathode Precursor Plant Finland. A 30% lithium price increase impacts NMC811 cell costs, flowing through to EV Battery Packs (OEM contracts with Tesla and European OEM) and Grid Storage Modules. OEM contract price adjustment clauses should be reviewed.
reasoningSteps: Lithium Carbonate from Chile and procured on Spot Market
Co-required with Nickel Sulfate for cathode synthesis
Both feed Cathode Precursor Plant Finland
30% price increase flows through to NMC811 cells
EV Battery Packs sold through OEM contracts to Tesla and European OEM
Grid Storage Modules sold to Grid Operator X
Contract price adjustment clauses need review

questions.question: IRA domestic content threshold increases to 80% next year. Which product lines are at risk?
questions.answer: Using the manufacturing cost method (not weight - common error that overestimates by 10-15%), EV Battery Pack 75kWh is at risk due to DRC cobalt and Chinese graphite sourcing. Grid Storage Module has the same exposure. The Cathode Precursor Plant in Finland helps (low-carbon energy), but upstream material origins from Chile, DRC, China, and Indonesia pull domestic content below 80%. Compliance Director gets remediation plan with cost estimates for each sourcing restructuring scenario.
reasoningSteps: Regulation Change Assessment triggers: IRA domestic content threshold updated to 80%.
Entity traversal: All product lines -> material origins -> supplier geographic locations.
Skill fires: "IRA Content Calculation Shortcut" (Department priority) - use manufacturing cost method, not weight. Recalculates both products.
Compliance Gap Remediation triggers: Both EV Battery Pack and Grid Storage Module fall below 80% due to imported material sourcing.
Skill fires: "EU Battery Passport Data Mapping" cross-checks smelter-level origin data for traceability.
People routed: Compliance Director gets gap remediation plan with cost-ranked restructuring scenarios.
